title: Custom trigger fails to activate with parameter error
symptom: When attempting to activate a workflow with a custom trigger, n8n displays 'Could not get parameter'. The trigger works fine when invoked manually.
not_this_if: Trigger works in manual execution mode
root_cause: The error occurs because the custom trigger is not properly configured to handle certain parameters during the workflow activation process. This can happen if the trigger expects parameters that are not provided or are incorrectly defined.
fix_steps: Modify the custom trigger code to ensure all required parameters are correctly defined
Rebuild and push the modified n8n application to your Docker instance
Recreate the Docker container to apply changes
agent_instruction: Ensure all parameters in the custom trigger are correctly defined and rebuild the n8n application.
